citations.description: Search for ne7642adbf79, n0eb3f2e9da9, access-snapshot-pre-autolander, infra/gerrit across tests/ — all returned no matches.

findings.criterion: Pass 3 drops a finding when claims_absence=yes and absence_confirmed_in_context=no; a regression fixture replaying the f1fa 11:44 false-absence finding is dropped, while a TRUE absence claim survives.
findings.detail: The absence-claim veto is implemented in decide.py (lines 333–341) and basic tests exist in test_review_kernel.py (line 89–97), test_plan_review.py (line 300–305), and test_a8e5_absence_veto_edge.py. However, the specific regression fixture replaying the f1fa 11:44 verdict (norm ne7642adbf79 + G3 n0eb3f2e9da9) is completely absent. A search across the entire tests/ tree finds no references to ne7642adbf79, n0eb3f2e9da9, access-snapshot-pre-autolander, or any fixture data tied to the described incident. The AC explicitly requires this named fixture.

findings.criterion: The DET-scoped hygiene backstop drops a subject-less DET finding … and a test proves the backstop never touches LLM-tier findings.
findings.detail: The backstop is implemented (det_floor.py:932–967) and the test in test_plan_review.py (lines 309–331) verifies subject-less DET findings drop. However, the AC requires 'a test proves the backstop never touches LLM-tier findings.' The test docstring states 'This NEVER touches LLM-tier findings (they don't flow through det_*_findings)' but this is a comment, not a behavioral assertion. No test explicitly sends a non-DET finding through the backstop and asserts it is unaffected. The structural separation (det_*_findings accept only DetResult objects, not plain dicts) makes the invariant true, but the AC requires an explicit test proof.

findings.criterion: The [operator-attested] tag is DET-detected and injected into severity_attributes; a tagged AC's in-session-unverifiability finding loses the ac_unverifiable hard-override contribution deterministically, with a test for a finding that still survives on other axes.
findings.detail: DET detection and injection are implemented in workflow_ops.py (lines 61–116) and correctly called in plan_review_decide (lines 421–428). The test_enrich_operator_attested_clears_ac_unverifiable_upstream test (test_plan_review.py:350–375) and the edge tests in test_a8e5_operator_attested_edge.py verify ac_unverifiable is cleared. However, 'a test for a finding that still survives on other axes' is absent: no test demonstrates that when ac_unverifiable is cleared for an OA-tagged finding, another non-zero axis (e.g., divergent_implementation=high) still produces impact and the finding survives. The 'sole basis' guarantee (ticket text) requires this explicit test.

citations.description: Search for 'operator-attested' across src/rebar/llm/reviewers/ returns only completion_verifier.md; passes.py MOVE_REGISTRY has no entry for operator-attested coaching.

findings.criterion: The E2/F1/E6 rubrics and one new coaching move carry the ADR-0043 vocabulary. Proving check for 'non-prescriptive': the new MOVE_REGISTRY entry passes the existing coach-move validation.
findings.detail: (1) The plan_review_E2.md, plan_review_F1.md, and plan_review_E6.md rubric prompts contain no reference to [operator-attested], ADR-0043, 'recorded attestation', or any related vocabulary. A search across all reviewers/ prompts for 'operator-attested' returns only completion_verifier.md — the plan-review rubric prompts (E2/F1/E6) are untouched. (2) The MOVE_REGISTRY in passes.py tops out at move id '13' ('realign to parent plan'); no new coaching move for 'state the concrete attestation evidence the tagged AC will require' is present. Both sub-requirements of AC5 are unmet.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
